bead.title: agent: implement validated replacement safe rewrites
bead.description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Context is king · Drift is debt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go changes live under cli/ · prefer focused unit tests with in-memory stubs for domain interfaces · run targeted go test plus lefthook · preserve audit evidence for automated mutations</practices>
<governing>FEAT-010 and ADR-023 now define safe rewrites as validated replacement rewrites: fit-for-purpose replacement is allowed when preservation is proven from durable anchors; original text belongs in readiness evidence, not duplicated into the bead prompt.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Safe rewrite implementation still enforces append-only description preservation even though the governing specs now require validated replacement rewrites. This keeps stale/noisy bead text in the implementation prompt and rejects useful replacements; it also fails the opposite case, where a one-line vague bead must expand into a standalone executable task.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:118-120 rejects any description rewrite unless the normalized old description appears as a substring of the new description.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:183-188 prompts the intake model to return rewrites but does not say replacement rewrites should optimize prompt fitness, validate against durable anchors, and preserve original text through evidence rather than prompt duplication.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:176-211 and docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:136-166 now require validated replacement rewrites, prompt fitness, and audit evidence.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:60-80 records before/after hashes but does not preserve enough original/replacement evidence or preservation rationale for audit when the old body is replaced.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the substring-only description guard with a deterministic validated-replacement policy. The validator must preserve explicit commitments: acceptance criteria, non-scope, governing artifact references, dependencies, named files/tests, and still-valid root-cause evidence, or replace stale evidence with current durable file:line/section anchors.
- Allow both directions of prompt-fitness improvement: expanding underspecified beads with necessary durable context, and compressing noisy/stale beads while preserving explicit commitments.
- Keep rejection behavior for invented behavior, changed scope, deleted unresolved constraints, or missing preservation evidence.
- Update the intake prompt to ask for fit-for-purpose replacement rewrites, durable preservation evidence, and explicit ambiguity instead of append-only amendments.
- Extend the `intake-rewritten` event body so original/replacement audit evidence and preservation rationale are available without embedding the old body back into the new prompt. Use hashes plus bounded excerpts or attachment pointers consistent with existing event-size conventions.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement child decomposition changes.
- Do not relax acceptance criteria preservation.
- Do not allow product-semantic choices to be made by the rewrite validator.
- Do not rename legacy `PreClaimIntake*` structs in this bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cd555c0. Depends on ddx-5cd555c0 so the spec-evolution bead lands first.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement verifies a replacement description can remove stale/noisy old prose when explicit commitments and durable anchors are preserved.
2. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsExpansionReplacement verifies a vague one-line description can expand into a standalone executable bead when added content is grounded in durable context and does not invent product semantics.
3. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RejectsDeletedCommitment verifies non-scope, governing artifact references, named files/tests, or unresolved constraints cannot be silently removed.
4. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RecordsReplacementEvidence verifies `intake-rewritten` records changed fields, rationale, before/after hashes, and preservation evidence sufficient to audit the original text without duplicating it into the replacement prompt body.
5. TestPreClaimIntakePrompt_AsksForFitForPurposeValidatedReplacement verifies the prompt tells the model to optimize prompt fitness, avoid append-only noise, and classify ambiguous cases as needs-human.
6. Existing TestIntake_ActionableButRewritten_UpdatesAfterClaim and TestIntake_UnsafeRewriteBlocksForHuman remain green.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite|TestIntake_.*Rewrite" -count=1 passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"rationale":"All 8 rubric criteria pass. (1) Title is imperative, names the subsystem (agent) and the change (validated replacement safe rewrites). (2) Root cause cites four file:line spans across two source files and two governing docs. (3) AC has 8 numbered items each tied to a specific verifiable outcome; five items name Test* functions or go test -run filters. (4) AC items 1–6 each encode a wired-in assertion about a distinct behavior. (5) AC 7 is the go test gate (cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run … -count=1) and AC 8 is the lefthook gate — correct terminal pair. (6) Labels carry phase:2, two area:* tags, kind:feature, reliability/bead-quality annotations, and spec cross-refs for FEAT-010 and ADR-023. (7) Parent (ddx-5cd555c0) and deps ([ddx-5cd555c0]) are explicit and consistent; ordering rationale is stated. (8) Description is fully standalone: no /tmp/* paths, no chat references; context-digest uses only repo-relative governing doc paths. A competent agent can identify the two files to edit and run the named tests without asking.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}

{"rationale":"All 8 rubric criteria satisfied. Title is imperative and names the subsystem and change precisely. Description is structured with explicit PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE (four distinct file:line anchors across two source files and two governing docs), PROPOSED FIX with concrete policy direction, and NON-SCOPE list. AC is numbered 1–8, names five new TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_* and one TestPreClaimIntakePrompt_* function, explicitly pins two existing regression tests in item 6, and closes with the required `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run ... -count=1` gate (item 7) and `lefthook run pre-commit passes` (item 8). Labels carry phase:2, two area tags, kind:feature, and two spec cross-refs (FEAT-010, ADR-023). Parent ddx-5cd555c0 is declared in both the parent field and the description with rationale; deps field is consistent. No /tmp references, no chat references, no out-of-band context. An agent can identify the files to edit (preclaim_intake_rewrite.go lines 60–80 and 118–120; preclaim_intake_hook.go lines 183–188), the policy to implement, and the tests to run without operator hand-curation.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}
